raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California consistently holds the title for highest gas prices in the nation.",
    "The average price of gasoline in California is up to $5.76 per gallon compared to a national average of $4.24 per gallon.",
    "The significant price difference between California and the national average is $1.52 per gallon.",
    "Gas prices in California have continued to rise due to Sacramento Democrats’ goal to eliminate California’s domestic production and increase foreign imports.",
    "Senate Bill 1 (Beall) signed in 2017 increased taxes and fees on gasoline and diesel by $52 billion over ten years.",
    "The law requires that the tax rates and fees be adjusted annually to reflect the California Consumer Price Index.",
    "An excise tax increase of 5.6% will take effect on July 1, raising gasoline prices further.",
    "The Department of Finance estimates that the July 1 excise tax increase will cost taxpayers an additional $523 million a year.",
    "Senator Grove's bill aimed to provide relief at the pump by stopping the annual inflation escalator on gas taxes."
  ]
}
```
